title: Commentary on Key Financial Performance – December 31, 2026
content:
Capital & Solvency
The credit union maintained a net worth ratio of 10.33%, reflecting an increase of 4 basis points from the prior quarter and 37 basis points year-over-year. The solvency evaluation remained at 1.10, unchanged from last quarter but up 26 basis points compared to the same period last year.

Asset Quality
The delinquency ratio stood at 0.34%, marking an increase of 10 basis points versus the prior quarter and a decrease of 9 basis points relative to the previous year end. The net charge-off ratio was recorded at 0.22% for the current period, representing a decline of 14 basis points from last quarter and a reduction of 27 basis points compared to the same quarter in the prior fiscal year.

Profitability
Return on average assets (ROAA) reached 0.73%, up 98 basis points sequentially and 28 basis points annually. Net interest margin expanded by 53 basis points over last quarter, reaching 3.35% for the period, while net operating expenses averaged 3.06% of average assets, a decrease of 25 basis points from the prior quarter.

Funding & Growth
The loans-to-shares ratio was reported at 78.75%, showing an increase in balance-sheet positioning relative to last year’s level and remaining stable versus the previous quarter. Loan growth registered at 5.47% for the period, while share growth stood at 4.99%. The core funding ratio remained constant at 24.68% compared with prior periods.

Closing
The credit union presents a mixed financial picture across its four key areas this period: capital strength remains robust and solvency has improved; asset quality shows some deterioration in delinquencies despite lower charge-offs; profitability is strong on both margins and ROAA, supported by reduced operating expenses; funding positioning continues to expand. Continued monitoring of the rising delinquency ratio will be important as it marks a notable increase from last quarter.
